title: Teenager develops Popcorn Lungs after years of vaping, know the symptoms, causes of the rare condition
summary: Popcorn lung is a condition which causes damage to your lungs' smallest airways, making you cough and feel short of breath. It is usually caused by breathing in a chemical that is found in e-cigarette vapour. Read on to know about the symptoms and causes of Popcorn Lungs.

title: Measles, beef fat, and bird flu: What Europeans say about RFK Jr’s Make America Healthy Again agenda
summary: European health authorities agree with Donald Trump’s health secretary on some big picture issues - but not on all the details.

title: What happens when you eat 2-3 dates daily
summary: Packed with essential nutrients like fiber, potassium, iron, and antioxidants, dates are said to support everything from digestion to heart health. While we all know that eating dates is a healthy diet practice, do you know what actually happens when you eat 2-3 dates daily? Scroll down to find out.

title: 3 reasons your blood sugar level is high despite quitting sugar
summary: Cut sugar but still have high blood sugar? Experts explain hidden carbs, stress, sleep, and underlying conditions impacting glucose levels. Learn how to manage it effectively.

title: AI tool to check for skin cancer rolled out at London hospital
summary: A London hospital says its AI technology can give patients the all-clear without seeing a doctor.

title: Jersey brain tumour patient 'not offered CT scan'
summary: Sophie Reid says she experienced a delay in her diagnosis because "processes weren't followed".

title: Treating India’s lifestyle diseases? Start with gut health, say experts
summary: As India grapples with the triple burden of malnutrition, obesity, and non-communicable diseases, experts say poor gut health may be silently undermining the country’s nutritional progress.

title: 'I thought I was too young for a bowel cancer diagnosis at 24'
summary: Pippa Slater says it is vital people do not ignore symptoms after her cancer was caught early.

title: Common respiratory infection triples the risk of death, study reveals
summary: A study reveals that Respiratory Syncytial Virus-associated Acute Respiratory Infection increases death risk in adults. The risk is three times higher
